title: Ashikaga Yoshiharu
content:
was the twelfth "shōgun" of the Ashikaga shogunate from 1521 through 1546 during the late Muromachi period of Japan. He was the son of the eleventh "shōgun" Ashikaga Yoshizumi. His childhood name was Kameomaru (亀王丸). Not having any political power and repeatedly being forced out of the capital of Kyoto, Yoshiharu retired in 1546 over a political struggle between Miyoshi Nagayoshi and Hosokawa Harumoto making his son Ashikaga Yoshiteru the thirteenth shogun.

From a western perspective, Yoshiharu is significant, as he was shogun in 1543, when the first contact of Japan with the Europe an West took place. A Portuguese ship, blown off its course to China, landed in Japan. Events of Yoshiharu's "bakufu". Significant events shape the period during which Yoshiharu was "shōgun": Eras of Yoshiharu's "bakufu". The years in which Yoshiharu was "shōgun" are more specifically identified by more than one era name or "nengō".
